methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Humble Robotics is a seed-stage transportation company founded by Eyal Cohen that has raised $24M to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of seven private peers, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft, with no direct public comparables available.

Given the early stage and sparse peer set, peer-median valuation could not be reliably applied. The seed-stage peer cohort lacked sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers—only zero same-stage and one adjacent-stage data point were identified, falling short of the three-point minimum for statistical confidence. Consequently, stage-progression analysis provided the primary valuation signal. Using the $24M total capital raised as a value proxy and applying a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage multiplier yields an implied value of $60M.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median of $60M. The range reflects modest sensitivity around the stage-progression methodology—the single applicable method limits triangulation robustness. Key uncertainties center on the thinness of comparable data in early-stage transportation technology and the absence of public market anchors. A more confident range would require either acquisition of additional seed-stage transportation comps or evidence of revenue or unit economics to support alternative valuation approaches.
